nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and seaward reefs, seagrass beds, and hard-bottomed habitats; often found among branching corals at depths from the surface to ~46 m.

diet.notes: In the wild, has been reported to feed nocturnally on small fishes, small crabs, and shrimps. Generally accepts a variety of meaty foods in captivity including chopped marine meats, mysis, krill, and frozen shrimp; live saltwater feeder shrimp are commonly recommended to entice initial feeding after introduction.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in its mouth

summary: A nocturnal Indo-Pacific reef squirrelfish reaching about 30 cm; generally tolerant of tankmates of its own size but tends to prey on small fish and invertebrates, and carries a venomous preopercular spine.
careNotes: The Sammara Squirrelfish is a nocturnal species and tends to hide in caves and overhangs during the day, becoming active at dusk. It can be kept singly or in small groups when introduced simultaneously to mitigate intraspecific aggression. A well-sealed lid is generally advised since squirrelfishes have been known to jump. Handling caution is warranted: the species carries a venomous preopercular spine that has been reported to inflict painful wounds, and additional opercular and dorsal spines easily catch in nets. Generally considered not strictly reef-safe — small fish, ornamental shrimp, serpent stars, and small worms tend to be consumed. Sources disagree significantly on minimum tank size: LiveAquaria and Quality Marine state 70 gal (~265 L), Reefapp lists 800 L, and Reeflex recommends 3000 L for advanced keepers; 500 L was chosen as a cautious round value reflecting the species' adult size (~30 cm), active swimming habits, and predator behavior. Adult size also varies across sources: LiveAquaria notes ~5 in in captivity, while FishBase, Quality Marine, Reefapp, and Reeflex confirm a wild max of ~32 cm (12.6 in). Care level is rated from easy (LiveAquaria) to advanced (Reeflex); intermediate is the averaged assessment. The species is documented as a protogynous Holocentridae (likely female-first sequential hermaphrodite based on family biology) but specific sex-change confirmation for Neoniphon sammara was not found in reviewed sources; not flagged in the typed field as a result.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Squirrelfish collection page; species-specific product page (https://www.liveaquaria.com/product/3351/) provided most husbandry detail. Tank size 70 gal, easy care level, peaceful temperament, reef-safe with caution.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed scientific name (Neoniphon sammara), family Holocentridae, order Holocentriformes, max TL 32 cm, temperature range 24.7–29°C, Indo-Pacific distribution from Red Sea/East Africa to Polynesia, nocturnal diet of small fish/crabs/shrimps, and venomous preopercle spine.

additional.name: Reefapp.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (32 cm), reef-safe with caution, schooling behavior, nocturnal habits, and listed minimum tank size of 800 L — significantly larger than LiveAquaria's 70 gal.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), tank size 70 gal, max size 12 in, nocturnal behavior, and venomous preopercular spine warning.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (32 cm), Indo-Pacific range/depth (to 46 m), nocturnal hunting behavior, and care difficulty (rated 'advanced'). Lists minimum tank size of 3000 L — substantially higher than other sources, justifying needs_review on tank size.
